titleStmt.title: Letter from 12 named individuals to 'the Public', undated
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Appeal to the Public for funds on behalf of the Widow Shanahan and her seven orphan children to enable her to rebuild her premises and restart her business. The appeal was made by 12 individuals (three Priests, six Justices of the Peace, and three others including a solicitor).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers 1916, NAI-CSO-RP-1916-5623-5526b

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
APPEAL FOR ASSISTANCE ON BEHALF OF THE
WIDOW SHANAHAN AND HER SEVEN HELPLESS
CHILDREN The undersigned hereby appeal to the Charitable
Public on behalf of the Widow Shanahan of PORTMAGEE, and Her
SEVEN Helpless Children, whose Premises were accidently
burned down on the 6th of November last, for Help to enable
her to rebuild her premises and restart her business. Her Premises and Stock thereon, which were not
insured were totally destroyed thereby reducing her from
comparative Comfort to dire distress. In this her extreme need we trust that our Call
will not be in vain and a generous Response will be given
to tide her over her present difficulties. Father O'Brien P. P. Father Costelloe C. C.
Father Keane P. P. Edward Fitzgerald J. P. John Nannix J. P.
William J. Leslie J. P. Timothy Galvin J. P.
Timothy O'Driscoll J. P. James Trant J. P. C. G. O'Connell., M.J. O'Sullivan Solicitor
J. Butler. C.C. SHANAHAN
T. HURLEY
(Hon. Secretaries) SUBSCRIPTIONS WILL BE RECEIVED BY EITHER
OF THE HON. SECRETARIES.
